item.description:
Fixes

  • Dragging a single capture from the recent strip to Finder now drops a real, full-resolution image file. It previously created a tiny broken alias instead of the actual picture.
  • The empty area around the image in the editor now behaves like empty canvas: clicking it deselects, and you can start a selection box out there and drag across the image. Previously clicks out there did nothing at all.
  • Dragging a selection box now selects only the objects it fully encloses, instead of everything it touches on the way past.
  • The Editor/Library/Settings switcher no longer offers an Icon and Text / Icon Only / Text Only menu on right-click. Picking one of those could leave a stray "View" label sitting under the switcher. (macOS 15 and later.)

Licensing

  • Direct edition: installing a Sealshot version released after your license's update window now shows a renewal banner and pauses new captures and recordings until you renew or switch back to a covered version. Everything already captured stays fully viewable, editable, and exportable.

Library

  • Right-click All Files in the Library sidebar to Export All Files… — bundles your whole library into one package you can AirDrop to another Mac and import (optionally passphrase-protected). Works like exporting a collection, just for everything.
  • Exporting a package now shows how many items are done (e.g. "Packaging 5 of 10…") instead of a time-left estimate.

Security

  • Your recovery key screen now has "Save Kit…" and "Print…" buttons, so you can keep a PDF copy of your recovery code — with instructions — on paper or in your own backup, not just copy it to the clipboard.
  • Every so often, Sealshot will ask you to confirm you still have your recovery code (about once every 3 months). This never happens while your library is locked or more than once per launch, and you can always say "Remind Me Later" or generate a fresh code on the spot.
  • The lock screen now has an "I can't unlock…" option for when you've lost both Touch ID access and your recovery code. It explains why Sealshot can't bypass encryption for you, then — after you type RESET to confirm — moves your encrypted captures to a Locked Archive and turns encryption off so you can keep using the app. Nothing is deleted, and archived captures can still be restored later if you find your recovery code.
  • Captures moved to your Locked Archive now show up as their own section in the Library, so you can see what's there instead of it being hidden on disk. They're read-only — shown dimmed with a lock badge — since Sealshot can't decrypt them without your recovery code, but you can still select them and reveal them in Finder.
  • Found your recovery code again? You can now restore the Locked Archive: a Restore… button in the Library's Locked Archive section (and in Settings → Privacy & Security) asks for the code and brings those captures back into your library, re-enabling encryption with the recovered key. If you went through more than one reset, each round's captures come back with that round's own code — items belonging to a different code stay safely in the archive, and nothing is ever deleted — even if you'd already set up encryption again in the meantime. The restore screen also warns you if some internal keys couldn't be brought back, instead of failing silently. If restoring an older code needs to set aside your current encryption setup to make room, the restore screen now tells you so, and reminds you to bring your current setup back with its own recovery code.
  • Some Locked Archive items can never come back through Restore… — ones set aside when you turned encryption off, rather than from a reset — and Sealshot's messaging now says so plainly instead of implying a code will restore them: the Library banner drops the Restore… button when nothing in the archive is actually restorable, and the restore screen's wording no longer promises a code will work for items left behind.
  • Successfully entering your recovery code — whether to restore a Locked Archive or unlock on a new Mac — now counts as verifying it, the same as using the periodic "Verify Your Recovery Code" prompt, so you won't be asked again sooner than necessary.
  • Fixed the recovery-verify nudge's "Generate New…" button sometimes not opening Settings → Privacy & Security when Settings was already open.

Other

  • Live Captures now get keywords and a summary that lists each captured window with a one-line description. Scenes taken before this update pick theirs up the next time you open them.
  • Sealshot now has a 14-day free trial. After it, your existing captures stay fully viewable, editable, and exportable — a license is needed only for new captures and recordings. Activate by opening your license file (or dropping it) in Settings → License. A banner shows when your trial is in its final week.
  • The Library and Settings tabs use a clean solid backdrop instead of the hex-pattern background.
  • Sealshot now tells you when capture summaries need Apple Intelligence turned on, instead of quietly hiding them — and says plainly when your Mac can't run them, so you know nothing is broken.
  • Turning Apple Intelligence on in System Settings while a capture is open now resumes its summary and keywords automatically as soon as you switch back to Sealshot — no more closing and reopening the capture to make it generate. Settings → On-Device AI also updates right away instead of showing stale status.

Recording

  • The recording HUD now shows a Recording/Paused label beside the status icon.
  • The record prompt now appears over full-screen apps, on the display being recorded.
  • Each recording shortcut stops only its own recording mode.
  • System and microphone audio are recorded at native 48 kHz for cleaner sound.
  • A finished recording is selected automatically in the recent-captures strip.

Editor

  • One app-wide undo timeline: images, videos, metadata edits, and selection are all undoable steps, and each undo names what it undid.
  • New Free Arrow tool — draw an arrow along any path, next to the straight Line Arrow.
  • Pen, line, arrow, shape, step, and text annotations can now have an outline (casing) color with its own width control.
  • Text tool overhaul: weight slider, per-tool color and opacity memory, visible text-box frame with resize dots, and typography remembered across launches.
  • Captures render and export color-faithful — no more lightened text.
  • Dragging a focused capture to Finder exports just the focus area.
  • Duplicate a whole capture from the editor, the strip, or the Library.
  • Extracted Data results appear in a single consolidated view; exports default to the image's name, and cancelling an extraction takes effect immediately.
  • Structured Data Extraction uses the enhanced on-device model when it's downloaded.
  • The toolbar and tabs freeze during blocking AI actions; navigating away cancels the action.
  • Fit Width / Fit Height no longer leave a stray scrollbar.
  • A remembered video reopens with its player panel and controls in place.

Library

  • Resize the Quick Look preview with the −/+ buttons, and grid tiles with ⌘+scroll.
  • Play a video straight from its play badge in the strip; clicking the thumbnail opens it paused.
  • Preview is available from the context menu, and the video action "Play" is now "Open".
  • Drag multiple captures to Finder with a progress bar and Cancel.
  • Empty Trash button clears the Trash in one click.
  • Multi-select stays fast in large libraries.

Security

  • Idle auto-lock now follows system-wide idle time, not just activity inside the app.
  • Menus and keyboard shortcuts are gated while the library is locked.
  • Turning encryption on or off shows reliable progress and no longer damages video packages.
  • Fixed a case where an interrupted recovery-key ceremony left the tab switcher disabled.

Capture & OCR

  • A cancelled capture no longer raises the editor over the app you were using.
  • OCR now recognizes non-Latin scripts, with automatic language detection.
  • Smart Redaction catches more: 2FA backup-code grids, role names, ticket IDs, masked card numbers, and street addresses.

Other

  • Deleted items are kept 7 days by default before being purged, and the retention setting now applies reliably to every trashed item.
  • Your own tags are preserved when Smart Keywords are regenerated.
  • Update dialogs use a cleaner centered-icon design matching the About panel.
